memberdef.definition: using MR::Vector< T, I >::value_type = typename std::vector<T>::value_type
memberdef.name: value_type

memberdef.definition: using MR::Vector< T, I >::reference = typename std::vector<T>::reference
memberdef.name: reference

memberdef.definition: using MR::Vector< T, I >::const_reference = typename std::vector<T>::const_reference
memberdef.name: const_reference

memberdef.definition: using MR::Vector< T, I >::iterator = typename std::vector<T>::iterator
memberdef.name: iterator

memberdef.definition: using MR::Vector< T, I >::const_iterator = typename std::vector<T>::const_iterator
memberdef.name: const_iterator

briefdescription.para: the user can directly manipulate the vector, anyway she cannot break anything
creates a vector with size elements with default value
creates a vector with size elements with the given value
moves data from the given std::vector<T>
resizes the vector skipping initialization of its elements (more precisely initializing them using ( noInit ) constructor )
doubles reserved memory until resize(newSize) can be done without reallocation

inbodydescription.para: This separate overload is needed as opposed to a value = T{} default argument, because if T isn't default-constructible, the parsed chokes on that.

briefdescription.para: doubles reserved memory until resize(newSize, value) can be done without reallocation

memberdef.argsstring: (I pos, size_t len, T val) MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(sizeof(T)>0 &&std
memberdef.name: autoResizeSet

detaileddescription.para: sets elements [pos, pos+len) to the given value, adjusting the size of the vector to include new elements; the elements in between old size and pos are also set to val (for faster implementation)

inbodydescription.para: add new elements with the given value
the number of the elements existing before function call to be changed
change the value of the elements existing before function call

briefdescription.para: sets the element #i to the given value, adjusting the size of the vector to include new element
this accessor automatically adjusts the size of the vector
returns the identifier of the first element
returns the identifier of the back() element
returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap
std::vector<T>-like container that requires specific indexing type,

parameterdescription.para: type of index (shall be convertible to size_t)
